text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Ellison, Marcus R.
DATE OF BIRTH: 1969
SEX: Male
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Oncology/Surgical Oncology Service]
CLINICAL HISTORY: 55-year-old male with a pigmented ulcerated nodular lesion of the upper back present for approximately 18 months, rapidly enlarging over the past 4-5 months. Concurrent right axillary lymphadenopathy on imaging. Clinical concern for malignant melanoma.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A:
  Designation: A
  Description: Skin, upper back, excisional biopsy (wide local excision with 1 cm margins)
  Procedure date: [date not recorded in this document]
  Submitted by: Surgical Oncology

Specimen B:
  Designation: B
  Description: Right axillary lymph node, core needle biopsy (ultrasound-guided, 3 cores submitted)
  Procedure date: [date not recorded in this document]
  Submitted by: Interventional Radiology

------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A - Skin, upper back, wide local excision:
Received in formalin is an elliptical excision of skin and subcutaneous tissue measuring 9.5 x 6.2 x 3.8 cm. The overlying epidermis demonstrates a centrally located, irregularly bordered, dark brown-to-black nodular lesion measuring 4.2 x 3.8 cm in greatest dimensions. The lesion is raised, multinodular, and has an ulcerated central surface measuring approximately 2.0 x 1.5 cm. Satellite pigmented papules are noted within 1.5 cm of the main tumor nodule. The cut surface reveals a dark, firm, glistening nodular mass extending from the dermal-epidermal junction through the dermis and into the subcutaneous fat; the deepest point of invasion appears to reach the deep resection margin. Excision margins: peripheral/radial margins are grossly uninvolved; the deep margin appears involved by gross tumor. Ink colors: deep margin black, peripheral margins blue. The specimen is serially sectioned at 2-3 mm intervals and submitted in entirety in cassettes A1 through A22. Representative sections of lesion including closest peripheral and deep margins submitted. Representative normal skin submitted.

Specimen B - Right axillary lymph node, core needle biopsy:
Received in formalin are three tan-white cylindrical tissue cores, each measuring approximately 1.5-1.8 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter. The cores are soft and homogeneous without grossly visible normal lymphoid tissue; the cut surface reveals a pale tan-white firm consistency throughout. All three cores are submitted in entirety in cassette B1.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A - Skin, upper back, wide local excision:
Histologic sections demonstrate a markedly atypical melanocytic proliferation arising in association with the epidermis and extending into the dermis and subcutaneous fat, consistent with nodular melanoma. The tumor is composed of sheets, nests, and fascicles of epithelioid melanocytes with abundant pale to amphophilic cytoplasm, large pleomorphic nuclei, and prominent, eosinophilic macronucleoli. A subset of cells displays a spindled morphology. Significant cytologic atypia is present throughout. Pigment production is heterogeneous; heavily pigmented and amelanotic areas are both present. The overlying epidermis shows ulceration over an area of approximately 2.0 x 1.5 cm with associated fibrinopurulent exudate and granulation tissue.

Bresl thickness (as measured by ocular micrometry): 6.1 mm from the granular layer (or from the base of ulceration) to the deepest point of tumor invasion.

Clark level of invasion: Level V (tumor cells present within and infiltrating the subcutaneous fat).

Ulceration: Present (confirmed microscopically, involving approximately 40% of the tumor surface).

Mitotic rate: 6 mitoses per mm2 (hot-spot method, counted in dermal component).

Perineural invasion: Not identified.

Lymphovascular invasion: Present; intravascular collections of melanoma cells are identified within dilated lymphovascular channels in the papillary and reticular dermis.

Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs): Non-brisk; scattered lymphocytic infiltrate at the base of the tumor.

Regression: Not identified.

Satellitosis: Microscopic satellite nodules present (discrete nests of melanoma cells separated from the main tumor mass by normal dermis or subcutaneous fat, within 2 cm).

Margins:
  - Deep margin: Positive; the neoplasm extends to the inked deep resection margin.
  - Peripheral/radial margins: Negative for melanoma; closest peripheral margin is approximately 8 mm from the nearest tumor extension.

Associated in situ component: No discrete melanoma in situ (lentigo maligna or superficial spreading) component identified at the periphery; findings are consistent with a primary nodular melanoma without a significant in situ precursor.

Background skin: Solar elastosis noted in the surrounding skin. No dysplastic nevi identified in the sections examined.

Specimen B - Right axillary lymph node, core needle biopsy:
Histologic sections of the core biopsies demonstrate complete effacement of the normal lymph node architecture by sheets of epithelioid and focally spindled atypical melanocytic cells, morphologically identical to the primary cutaneous lesion. Residual lymphoid tissue is not identified within the sampled cores. Brisk mitotic activity is present. Areas of geographic necrosis are seen. Extranodal extension is identified at the core biopsy edge (representing the true nodal capsule and perinodal adipose tissue), with neoplastic cells infiltrating into perinodal fibroadipose tissue beyond the nodal capsule.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Specimen A, primary melanoma, block A7; and Specimen B, lymph node core, block B1):

Melanocytic markers:
  S100 protein:             POSITIVE (diffuse, strong nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in both specimens)
  HMB-45 (gp100):          POSITIVE (strong, heterogeneous cytoplasmic staining, both specimens)
  Melan-A (MART-1):         POSITIVE (strong cytoplasmic staining, both specimens)
  SOX10:                    POSITIVE (diffuse nuclear staining, both specimens)

Proliferation:
  Ki-67:                    Approximately 40% (nuclear positivity, Specimen A); similarly elevated in Specimen B

BRAF V600E-specific immunohistochemistry:
  VE1 antibody (BRAF V600E): STRONGLY POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in both specimens)
  Interpretation: Result is consistent with the presence of a BRAF p.V600E missense mutation. Confirmatory molecular testing is recommended and has been ordered (see below).

PD-L1 expression:
  PD-L1 (28-8 pharmDx assay, Specimen A primary tumor):
    Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 25%
    Combined Positive Score (CPS): 38
    Interpretation: PD-L1 positive (TPS >=1%). Result is scored by a board-certified pathologist using the FDA-approved 28-8 assay scoring algorithm.

Other markers:
  Pancytokeratin (AE1/AE3):  NEGATIVE (excludes carcinoma)
  Desmin:                    NEGATIVE
  CD34:                      NEGATIVE

MOLECULAR STUDIES:
  Next-generation sequencing (comprehensive solid tumor panel, 523-gene) from Specimen B (right axillary lymph node core, FFPE): ORDERED - RESULT PENDING. Expected turnaround 10-14 business days. Report will be issued as an addendum.

  BRAF mutational analysis (PCR/NGS confirmation): ORDERED - RESULT PENDING (to confirm VE1 IHC result; included in NGS panel above).

  Germline/somatic distinction: To be determined pending NGS results and clinical correlation.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY
------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN A - SKIN, UPPER BACK, WIDE LOCAL EXCISION:
  NODULAR MELANOMA
  - Breslow thickness: 6.1 mm
  - Clark level: V (invasion of subcutaneous fat)
  - Ulceration: Present
  - Mitotic rate: 6/mm2
  - Lymphovascular invasion: Present
  - Perineural invasion: Absent
  - Microsatellitosis: Present
  - Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes: Non-brisk
  - Deep margin: POSITIVE
  - Peripheral margins: NEGATIVE (closest 8 mm)
  - BRAF V600E IHC (VE1): Strongly positive
  - PD-L1 TPS (28-8): 25%; CPS: 38
  - Ki-67: ~40%
  Pathologic T stage (AJCC 8th edition): pT4b (thickness >4 mm with ulceration)

SPECIMEN B - RIGHT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
  METASTATIC MELANOMA
  - Complete effacement of nodal architecture
  - Extranodal extension: Present
  - BRAF V600E IHC (VE1): Strongly positive
  - Ki-67: ~40%
  Note: Core needle biopsy does not permit formal pN staging (lymph node count not assessable on core biopsy); staging to be correlated with clinical and radiographic findings.

OVERALL IMPRESSION:
Findings are diagnostic of a high-risk primary nodular melanoma of the upper back (pT4b) with confirmed metastatic disease to the right axillary lymph node. The combination of Breslow thickness >6 mm, ulceration, high mitotic rate, lymphovascular invasion, microsatellitosis, positive deep margin, and nodal metastasis with extranodal extension is consistent with advanced-stage melanoma (clinical Stage IV in the context of imaging findings). BRAF V600E expression confirmed by VE1 immunohistochemistry in both primary and metastatic specimens; molecular confirmation pending. PD-L1 expression is positive by 28-8 assay. Additional molecular profiling results pending as addendum.

ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED BY:
[Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

ADDENDUM NOTE: Comprehensive NGS report and BRAF confirmatory molecular analysis results to be issued as a separate addendum upon receipt of results from the molecular pathology laboratory.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
QUALITY ASSURANCE NOTE: All immunohistochemical stains were performed with appropriate positive and negative controls. PD-L1 28-8 assay performed and scored per FDA-approved guidelines. VE1 (BRAF V600E) antibody interpretation performed in the context of appropriate positive (colorectal carcinoma with known V600E mutation) and negative controls.
------------------------------------------------------------------------
